title: Coffee and Tea Help Keep Your Brain Healthy
summary: A big study that lasted for 43 years found that drinking coffee or tea can help protect your brain. Scientists looked at over 130,000 people and discovered that those who drank 2-3 cups of coffee or 1-2 cups of tea each day had a lower chance of getting dementia, which is a brain disease that makes it hard to remember things. The study showed that these drinks might help keep your brain working well as you get older. The researchers think that caffeine in coffee and tea could be the reason for this benefit. They also found that it doesn't matter if you have a family history of dementia; coffee and tea can still help. This is good news because there aren't many ways to prevent dementia, so drinking these beverages might be a simple thing you can do. The study was done by doctors from big hospitals and schools like Harvard, and they used data from long-term health studies to get these results.

keywords.explanation: a brain disease that makes it hard to remember and think clearly
a substance in coffee and tea that can give you energy
research done by scientists to learn something new
how well your brain works and stays strong
doing things to stop something bad from happening

How many cups of coffee per day showed the best brain protection?
What brain disease did the study look at?
Which drink did NOT show the same brain benefits in the study?
What might be the helpful substance in coffee and tea?

background_read: Dementia is a condition where people have trouble remembering things and thinking clearly as they get older. It can make daily life hard. Scientists are always looking for ways to prevent it because there aren't good cures yet. Coffee and tea are popular drinks that many people enjoy every day. This study wanted to see if drinking them could help keep brains healthy. The researchers used information from big health studies that tracked people for many years to find out.
Article_Structure: WHO: Doctors from hospitals and schools like Harvard studied over 130,000 people.
WHAT: They found that drinking 2-3 cups of coffee or 1-2 cups of tea daily can lower the risk of dementia.
WHERE: The study was done using data from long-term health studies in the United States.
WHY: To see if simple drinks like coffee and tea could help prevent brain diseases as people age.

perspectives.description: Drinking coffee and tea might be a good way to protect your brain from diseases like dementia.
